gate_question: Does this exact current protocol, with current live state, allow an unprivileged attacker to extract funds or rewards beyond entitlement?

candidate.title_or_claim: updateTokenPoolInfo lacks role check at Portal.sol layer; role enforcement unverified in PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER delegatecall target
candidate.claimed_root_cause: Portal.sol applies only onlyIfBitFlagsSet(CB_BIT_MASK_GLOBAL_SWITCH) on updateTokenPoolInfo; DEX_ROUTER_MANAGER_ROLE enforcement exists only in the unindexed PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER delegatecall target
candidate.claimed_impact: Unprivileged attacker overwrites _dexPools[token] with a fake pool, causing subsequent swapExactInput calls on DEX-status tokens to route to attacker-controlled pool and drain user quote tokens

live_preconditions.name: portal_not_halted
live_preconditions.observed_value: live_context confirms proxy is active; bitFlags value not directly read but proxy is operational

live_preconditions.name: dex_status_token_exists
live_preconditions.observed_value: not confirmed — LaunchedToDEX event keys not decoded in live_context

live_preconditions.name: portal_dex_router_address
live_preconditions.observed_value: not confirmed — PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER immutable value not read from live proxy

live_preconditions.name: portal_dex_router_lacks_role_check
live_preconditions.observed_value: unverified — PortalDexRouter source not present in src/ or out/ directories; DEX_ROUTER_MANAGER_ROLE not defined in PortalBase.sol constants (unlike all other roles); role only appears in IPortal.sol interface comment

source_code_basis.why_it_matters: Entry point applies only onlyIfBitFlagsSet(CB_BIT_MASK_GLOBAL_SWITCH) — a liveness guard — then unconditionally delegates to PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER. No role check at this layer.
Confirms the modifier only checks the global feature bit; it is not an access-control guard.
_dexPools mapping stores PackedDexPool per token; overwriting it redirects swap routing for DEX-status tokens.

All other roles (GUARDIAN_ROLE, TAX_MANAGER_ROLE, TAX_GUARDIAN_ROLE, TOKEN_FLAP_FEE_SETTER_ROLE, MODERATOR_ROLE) are defined here as public constants. DEX_ROUTER_MANAGER_ROLE is absent — it is only referenced in the IPortal.sol interface comment, suggesting it may be defined and enforced exclusively inside the PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER implementation.
Interface comment states 'can only be called by DEX_ROUTER_MANAGER_ROLE roles' — documents intended restriction but does not constitute enforcement.
Confirms that DEX-status tokens route swapExactInput to PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER, which reads _dexPools[token]. A corrupted pool entry would misdirect swap proceeds.

attacker_inputs: token: address of a live DEX-graduated token (TokenStatus.DEX)
poolInfo.pool: attacker-controlled fake pool contract address
call_sequence: 1. Attacker calls Portal.updateTokenPoolInfo(dexToken, fakePoolInfo) — passes onlyIfBitFlagsSet check
2. Portal._delegateToImpl(PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER) executes PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER bytecode in Portal storage context
3. IF PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER omits hasRole(DEX_ROUTER_MANAGER_ROLE, msg.sender): writes _dexPools[dexToken] = fakePoolInfo
5. _getSwapImplementation returns PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER; swap delegates to PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER
6. PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER reads _dexPools[dexToken].pool = fakePool; routes swap to fake pool
7. Fake pool receives victim's quote tokens and returns zero or dust tokens to victim
state_after: subsequent swaps on dexToken route to fake pool

extraction_analysis.asset_or_reward: User quote tokens (BNB or ERC-20) sent during swapExactInput on DEX-status tokens
extraction_analysis.attacker_gain: Quote tokens received by fake pool contract controlled by attacker
extraction_analysis.victim_or_protocol_loss: Swap users lose quote tokens; receive zero or dust base tokens in return
extraction_analysis.why_gain_exceeds_entitlement: Attacker has no entitlement to other users' swap proceeds; the role restriction documented in the interface is intended to prevent exactly this redirection

setup: Fork BSC at block 108382650 against proxy 0xe2ce6ab80874fa9fa2aae65d277dd6b8e65c9de0
Query LaunchedToDEX events to obtain a live dexToken address
Deploy FakePool contract that records callers and returns attacker address on token queries
Create unprivileged attacker EOA (vm.addr(1)) with no roles
transaction_sequence: Optionally: vm.prank(victim); portal.swapExactInput(quoteToken, dexToken, amount, 0, deadline)
expected_assertions: PROTECTED: call reverts with 'AccessControl: account 0x... is missing role 0x...'
VULNERABLE: call succeeds; portal.getTokenV8(dexToken).dexPool == address(fakePool); subsequent swap routes to fakePool
local_proof_required.reject_if_assertion_fails: If updateTokenPoolInfo reverts with an access-control error for the unprivileged EOA, the PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER enforces the role and the candidate must be REJECTED.

rejection_reason: Cannot be promoted beyond NEEDS_LOCAL_PROOF: (1) PortalDexRouter.sol source is absent from the repository — the role enforcement point is entirely inside the unindexed delegatecall target; (2) DEX_ROUTER_MANAGER_ROLE is absent from PortalBase.sol constants (where all other roles are defined), which is consistent with the role being defined and enforced inside PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER itself rather than being missing; (3) no confirmed DEX-status token address is available from live_context to establish the precondition; (4) exact_code_path_exists and concrete_fund_or_reward_gain cannot be set true without reading the PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER bytecode or source. A BSC fork test is mandatory to determine whether the role check is present or absent.
